channel.title: Responsible AI & Neurodivergence — Naba Rizvi
channel.description: Curated research and news on ethical / responsible AI for neurodivergent people.

item.title: AI psychosis: mechanisms, clinical risks and safety considerations in generative AI chatbots (BJPsych Open, 2026)
item.description: Sycophancy chief among three chatbot traits that combine over long use to help vulnerable users co-build personalized delusions. Risk rises with family history of psychosis, sleep loss and isolation.

item.title: Potentially harmful consequences of AI chatbot use among patients with mental illness (PMC, 2026)
item.description: Early service-level data on adverse chatbot interactions among patients already in psychiatric care, a rare look at real clinical populations rather than general users.

item.title: Exploring implicit perspectives on autism in large language models through multi-agent simulations (arXiv)
item.description: Multi-agent simulations surface the implicit, deficit-framed assumptions LLMs hold about autistic people, a method you can reuse for auditing neurodivergence bias.

item.title: Simulating psychological risks in human-AI interactions: real-case informed modeling (arXiv)
item.description: Risk models built from documented real cases across psychosis, addiction, anorexia and suicide, a framework for stress-testing systems against serious-mental-illness harms.

item.title: Addressing disability inclusion in AI product organizations (arXiv)
item.description: Organizational study of how disability inclusion gets sidelined inside AI product teams, connecting responsible-AI rhetoric to the labor that actually produces it.

item.title: Researchers identify 3 key drivers behind 'AI psychosis' (Gizmodo, 2026)
item.description: Accessible write-up of the June 2026 findings, including why sycophancy is described as the most consequential driver.

item.title: Psychiatrists hope chat logs can reveal the secrets of AI psychosis (UCSF, Jan 2026)
item.description: UCSF clinicians are analyzing patient chat logs to understand how prolonged AI interaction interacts with psychotic illness.

item.title: Patients are bringing AI to therapy (American Psychological Association, 2026)
item.description: APA report finding 89% of psychologists worry chatbots may inadvertently encourage self-harm or miss crisis signals.

item.title: Addictive algorithms and the Digital Fairness Act (Petrie-Flom, Harvard Law, 2026)
item.description: Analyzes the EU's forthcoming Digital Fairness Act and how its treatment of manipulative design intersects with mental health and vulnerability.

item.title: Governing AI in mental health: a 50-state legislative review (JMIR Mental Health)
item.description: Comprehensive review of US state-level legislation governing AI in mental health, a reference map for a fragmented landscape.

item.title: AI to support neurodivergent learners in vocational education and training (OECD, 2026)
item.description: New OECD report on AI for learners with autism, ADHD, dyslexia, dyscalculia and dysgraphia, through a responsible-design lens.
